content: Do not delete adapter_defaults-duplicated safety keys (dispatch_ceiling.keywords, epic_autopilot.sensitive_keywords, epic_autopilot.hard_exclude_paths) from config.yaml to de-duplicate against adapter_defaults.DEFAULTS. scheduler.sh reads config.yaml via yq with no Python fallback; an absent key resolves to an empty string, and an empty grep -E pattern matches everything, silently defeating the dispatch-ceiling and sensitive-keyword safety gates. Keep the yaml copy and guard it with a byte/value-equality parity test against adapter_defaults.DEFAULTS instead (see #184 spec).

scripts/architecture_slice.py, diff_rank.py, gate_blast_radius.py (direct children of scripts/) and factory_core/*.py (relative imports) do not have a sys.path problem importing factory_core.adapter_defaults — verified empirically by invoking them from an unrelated CWD. Python auto-inserts a script's own directory (not CWD) at sys.path[0], and factory_core/ is a direct subdirectory of scripts/. Only factory_core/cli.py needs an explicit sys.path.insert, because it is nested inside factory_core/ yet imports itself via the absolute dotted path (from factory_core.board import ...). Do not add sys.path bootstrapping to the other files to 'fix' an import-fallback removal — there is nothing to fix (see #184 spec).
